[C94852] Living Arrangement -- **Semantic type:** Conceptual Entity  **Definition:** A description of a personal residence and the relationship between the occupants that reside there.  **Synonyms:** - Living Arrangements
[C94853] Anti-NMDA Receptor Encephalitis -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An autoimmune acute encephalitis caused by antibodies against the glutamate NMDA receptor.  It usually affects females and in the majority of cases it is associated with the presence of a tumor, most commonly an ovarian teratoma.  The presence of a tumor in patients with this form of encephalitis implies that the latter is a paraneoplastic syndrome.  It is manifested with psychiatric symptoms and epileptic seizures.  It is a potentially lethal disorder; however, it is usually reversible with the prompt removal of the tumor.
[C94854] Particle Accelerator -- **Semantic type:** Manufactured Object  **Definition:** An instrument that uses electric fields to propel charged particles to high speeds and energies.
[C94855] Concentration of Radioactive Activity -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The amount of radioactivity in a unit.
[C94856] Biological Half-Life -- **Semantic type:** Phenomenon or Process  **Definition:** The time that it takes for an unadulterated administered substance to be reduced by a factor of 2 in its concentration by natural physiological processes. This is entirely separate from any radioactive decay that may or may not be present.
[C94857] Long Axis -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** Line through and in the direction of an object's characteristic feature that is longer than any other feature.
[C94858] Acquisition Time Frame -- **Semantic type:** Temporal Concept  **Definition:** Temporal specification associated with a frame. Beginning and ending frame times after the start of injection completely specify an acquisition time.
[C94859] Anger Camera -- **Semantic type:** Medical Device  **Definition:** The original gamma camera invented by H. O. Anger in 1957 and its subsequent generations. This original camera consisted of one large crystal in which gamma rays were converted to scintillations of light. Then, a limited number of detectors viewed these through a collimator to provide an image.
[C9486] Anosognostic Epilepsy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A form of epilepsy in which an individual is unaware of their condition.
[C94860] Asymmetry Index -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A normalized measure of a difference between 2 quantities, expressed as a percentage difference of either their mean or their sum, whichever is defined by a user.
[C94861] Attenuation Coefficient -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The fraction of beam intensity being lost per unit length of the transmitting material in a chosen x direction.
[C94862] Attenuation Correction -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Adjustment for the effect of tissue thicknesses through which gamma's travel from an activity concentration origin to the scanner's detectors. The count rates of a detector are adjusted to values as if there were no attenuation along the gamma's path. This makes possible accurate quantitation in image analysis.  **Synonyms:** - ATTCRCT - Attenuation Correction Type - Attenuation Correction Type
[C94863] Axial Field of View -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** The distance along the body axis which can be imaged at one bed position by the scanner's detector geometry.
[C94864] Beta Particle -- **Semantic type:** Element, Ion, or Isotope  **Definition:** An electron or positron.  **Synonyms:** - BETA PARTICLE
[C94865] Blank Radiographic Scan -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** Scan without any subject or attenuating material, but with a source, present.  **Synonyms:** - Blank Scan
[C94866] Blood Flow Rate -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The volume of blood per unit time passing through a specified location, such as a point in a blood vessel or an entire organ. Units are ml/sec.  **Synonyms:** - BLDFLRT - BLDFLRT - BLDFLRT - BLDFLRT
[C94867] Blood Volume Fraction -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The portion of tissue occupied by blood. It is also sometimes expressed for a region as the volume of blood per unit mass of tissue.
[C94868] Brodmann's Area -- **Semantic type:** Body Location or Region  **Definition:** One of 47 numbered regions within a map of the cerebral cortex. Each is characterized by a particular cell organization and also corresponds to a particular cortical function.
[C94869] Cerebral Blood Flow -- **Semantic type:** Organ or Tissue Function  **Definition:** Perfusion of blood through the cerebrum.  **Synonyms:** - CBF
[C9487] Intractable Epilepsy -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** Epilepsy that is refractory to treatment.
[C94870] Cine Display Mode -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** A display mode in which the object appears to move due to seeing views from sequentially slightly differing angles. This contrasts with the usual static image display and can assist in visual interpretation.  **Synonyms:** - Cine
[C94871] Circumferential Profile Plot -- **Semantic type:** Activity  **Definition:** A plot whose ordinate is a quantifier, such as some measure of activity concentration, at points around an annulus in a slice, and whose abscissa is in degrees.  **Synonyms:** - Circumferential Profile
[C94872] Color Lookup Table -- **Semantic type:** Intellectual Product  **Definition:** A table used for interpreting pixels (voxels) to find the correspondence between image intensity values and displayed hues for the particular color scale employed.
[C94873] Compartmental Model Region -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** A distinct region or state for a particular chemical substance at a cellular level taken to have uniformity throughout but differing from other regions or states.  **Synonyms:** - Compartment - Compartment Region
[C94874] Compartmental Model -- **Semantic type:** Intellectual Product  **Definition:** A physiological model of the kinetics of uptake involving interchanges, such as of an isotope in a tracer, between compartments used to determine rate constants, such as by fitting dynamic scan data to this model.
[C94875] Conjugate View -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** 2 views in planar imaging that are acquired 180 degrees apart around the subject, such as anterior and posterior views.  **Synonyms:** - Conjugate Views
[C94876] Coronal Plane -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** Pertaining to a plane containing a line parallel to the long axis of the subject's body and in addition presenting anterior and posterior views on each side of it. This plane is perpendicular to both the transaxial and sagittal.  **Synonyms:** - Coronal
[C94877] Count per Second -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A unit of recurring events expressed as a number of events per second.
[C94878] Count Rate -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The number of discrete events, i.e. counts, which occur per unit time.
[C94879] Counting Efficiency -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The observed count rate divided by that expected if all events were counted.
[C94880] CT Number -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A normalized quantifier of the attenuation coefficient which is imaged in a CT scanner. The resulting encountered values are Hounsfield units and range from -1000 for air to + several hundred for very dense tissue, with water being zero.
[C94881] Cyclotron -- **Semantic type:** Manufactured Object  **Definition:** A member of the class of particle accelerator devices in which charged particles are accelerated electrically to high speeds while kept confined in a circular path by a strong electromagnet.
[C94882] Decay Constant -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** For a quantity Q(t) continually diminishing in value, the fraction being lost per unit time, namely -(dQ/dt)/ Q.  **Synonyms:** - Disintegration Constant
[C94883] Diagnostic Imaging Bull's Eye -- **Semantic type:** Conceptual Entity  **Definition:** A color scale or grayscale display of polar maps as concentric annuli.  **Synonyms:** - Bull's Eye
[C94884] DICOM Header Tag -- **Semantic type:** Intellectual Product  **Definition:** A uniquely defined quantity appearing in the computer file created according to the DICOM standard of transmitting images. It is important for the quantification of image features that many numerical values associated with the scanner configuration and protocol used be given. Each header tag is one such value.
[C94885] Disintegration per Second -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A unit of radioactive decay expressed in atoms of radioactive material that decay over a period of time equal to one second.  **Synonyms:** - dps
[C94886] Disintegration Rate -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The number of radioactive transformations per unit time and expressed as disintegrations per minute or disintegrations per second. It is the unit of measure for quantities of radionuclide.
[C94887] Dose Calibrator -- **Semantic type:** Manufactured Object  **Definition:** Device which quantifies an amount of radioactive substance. It would give the number of Becquerels or Curies present at a particular noted time.
[C94888] Dual-time Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** A scan which acquires two acquisitions of the same regions some time apart.
[C94889] Dynamic Range -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The largest difference between, or ratio of, an instrument's or method's highest and lowest value in a set, such as pixel (voxel) values in an image.
[C9489] Genitourinary Fistula -- **Semantic type:** Anatomical Abnormality|Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** An abnormal tract between the urinary system and the anterior surface of the gynecologic structures. These include vesico-vaginal, urethro-vaginal, uretero-vaginal, vesico-uterine, uretero-uterine, vesico-cervical, vesico-utero-vaginal fistulae.  **Synonyms:** - Urogenital Fistula
[C94890] Dynamic Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** A scan having a number of sequential frames.  **Synonyms:** - Dynamic Acquisition - Dynamic Imaging
[C94891] Electronic Collimation -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** The use of coincidence with a pair of gammas giving a coincidence event in detectors 180 degrees apart in a PET scanner to define a line of response along which lies the location of the radiation emitting tracer. This differs from the use of slab collimators whose many holes define lines of response from a single gamma emission.
[C94892] Emission Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** The usual mode of scanner operation in which a tracer's distribution throughout a subject is detected for subsequent processing into an image.
[C94893] End Diastolic Volume Imaging -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** Imaged ventricular blood pool capacity within the endocardial surface in a gated scan during the time of diastole.  **Synonyms:** - EDV
[C94894] End Systolic Volume Imaging -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** Imaged ventricular blood pool capacity within the endocardial surface in a gated scan during the time of systole.  **Synonyms:** - ESV
[C94895] Filter Cutoff Frequency -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Characteristic of a filter that gives the upper limit for the frequency content that essentially passes through it.
[C94896] Filtered Backprojection -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** A method of reconstruction that sums individual contributions to a pixel (voxel) value from detected counts of all lines of response passing through this pixel (voxel). It is an alternative approach to iterative reconstruction. Associated with this process is also a filtering that removes undesirable artifacts.  **Synonyms:** - Backprojection
[C94897] Flood Image -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** The image of a flood source.
[C94898] Flood Source -- **Semantic type:** Manufactured Object  **Definition:** A uniform distribution of a radioisotope in a large container which can be placed in an imaging device for purposes of quality control checking.
[C94899] Focal Image -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** Any limited region within a subject's image which visually stands out from its surroundings.
[C949] Zinc Oxide -- **Semantic type:** Inorganic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Synonyms:** - C.I. Pigment White 4 - ZINC OXIDE - zinc oxide
[C9490] Sarcoidal Granuloma -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A granuloma that develops due to sarcoidosis.
[C94900] Fourier Analysis -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A mathematical formalism that transforms sequences of values into an equivalent representation that consists of the sum of many sinusoids having different frequencies and amplitudes.
[C94901] Fourth Dimension -- **Semantic type:** Temporal Concept  **Definition:** Denoting a process that involves all 3 spatial dimensions in addition to some type of time information.  **Synonyms:** - 4D
[C94902] Frame Gate -- **Semantic type:** Temporal Concept  **Definition:** A selection of time intervals for frames based on special physiologic criteria.
[C94903] Full Width at Half Maximum -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A measure of resolution used when a system's response y (such as image-displayed activity concentration) to an ideal infinitely narrow spatially distributed input shows a width in its values surrounding a maximum when plotted against a locating variable x (such as distance relative to this input location).  **Synonyms:** - FWHM
[C94904] Fused Image -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** Results of two imaging modalities after a registration process is used to merge these. Their images can be displayed with different colors as if one were on top of the other.
[C94905] Gated Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** The acquisition of images that utilize only categorized scanner's counts during one or more (up to all) selected parts of some cyclic process in the subject.
[C94906] Geometric Mean -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The n-th root of the multiple products of n values, as contrasted with the normally understood arithmetic mean.  **Synonyms:** - MEAN, GEOMETRIC
[C94907] Horizontal Long Axis of Heart -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** An oblique plane through the heart containing its long axis and showing all 4 chambers. This and any plane parallel to it can be called a horizontal long axis view. These slices are perpendicular to vertical long axis views as well as short axis views.  **Synonyms:** - Horizontal Long Axis
[C94908] Hounsfield Unit -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Term given to the dimensions of the quantifier of x-ray attenuation, CT number.  **Synonyms:** - HU - HU - Hounsfield unit - Hounsfield unit - [hnsf'U] - [hnsf'U] - [hnsf'U] - [hnsf'U] - hnsf'U
[C94909] Hybrid Imaging -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** Use of a common bed for 2 imaging modalities implemented at or near the same time.
[C9491] Schistosome Granuloma -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A granuloma that develops in response to schistosomiasis.
[C94910] Image Reorientation -- **Semantic type:** Intellectual Product  **Definition:** Presentation of image views other than the standard coronal, sagittal and transaxial views.  **Synonyms:** - Reorientation
[C94911] Image Reslicing -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** Changing from one set of 2D slices to another set. The latter may have a different thickness and/or cut through the subject at a different angle than the original set.
[C94912] Image Spill-in -- **Semantic type:** Qualitative Concept  **Definition:** Phenomenon in a reconstructed image where true activity concentration of the tissue immediately adjacent to an object being studied, not displayed totally in its own corresponding pixels (voxels), appears spatially smeared into pixels (voxels) of this object.
[C94913] Image Spillover -- **Semantic type:** Qualitative Concept  **Definition:** Phenomenon in a reconstructed image where true activity concentration of an object is not displayed totally in its corresponding pixels (voxels). Instead it is spatially smeared into nearby pixels (voxels) corresponding to its surrounding medium.
[C94914] Imaging Cold Spot -- **Semantic type:** Qualitative Concept  **Definition:** A region of low activity within a larger region.
[C94915] Influx Constant -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A fractional rate by which tracer leaves plasma and is being trapped in a cell. Commonly it is obtained from the slope of a Patlak plot, where it is the increase in tissue activity concentration normalized by plasma activity concentration that occurs in a unit of normalized time.
[C94916] Infusion Rate -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The volume of a substance injected into a subject in a unit time.
[C94917] Injected Dose -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** The activity of a tracer injected at the beginning of a scan.  **Synonyms:** - ID
[C94918] Iterative Reconstruction -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** A method of reconstruction used in PET and SPECT that repeats a series of calculation steps with image data such that there is an improvement in the image quality in each repetition. It is an alternative approach to filtered backprojection.
[C94919] Least Square -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A popular criterion used for a cost function, namely minimizing the sum of the squares of the differences between the data points and the analytical function (often a straight line) desired as a best fit.
[C9492] Breast Ductal Hyperplasia -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** Hyperplasia of the epithelial cells in the breast ducts. It includes the atypical ductal hyperplasia, papillary intraductal hyperplasia, intraductal myoepitheliosis, and ductal hyperplasia of the usual type.  **Synonyms:** - Breast Epithelial Hyperplasia - Ductal Breast Hyperplasia - Ductal Hyperplasia of Breast - Ductal Hyperplasia of the Breast - Epithelial Hyperplasia of Breast - Epithelial Hyperplasia of the Breast
[C94920] Line of Response -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** A line defined by a detector and its collimator in SPECT or by 2 coincident detectors in PET.  **Synonyms:** - LOR
[C94921] Line Pair -- **Semantic type:** Idea or Concept  **Definition:** A pair within a sequence of identical pairs of parallel lines of equal thickness, one white and one black. This uniformly alternating intensity of such a test object can be used to visually assess the resolution of an imaging device.
[C94922] Linear Parameter Identification -- **Semantic type:** Idea or Concept  **Definition:** A consequence of a mathematical model in which all parameters appear as separate data coefficients to the first power in the equations used to evaluate these from fitting the model to data.
[C94923] List Mode -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** A data acquisition approach in which detector counts are stored sequentially as scan time progresses. This contrasts with the much more compact storage of time-averaged data in defined frames having pre-selected times and durations.
[C94924] Logan Plot -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** Plotting integrals of tissue activity concentrations Q and of plasma activity concentrations Cp, but these integrals both being tissue-normalized.
[C94925] Logarithm -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** For a specified base b, a function such that its argument results when b is raised to the power given by this function's value.  **Synonyms:** - log
[C94926] Macroparameter -- **Semantic type:** Conceptual Entity  **Definition:** A combination of fundamental parameters, such as would involve products, sums and quotients.
[C94927] Mathematic Identifiability -- **Semantic type:** Qualitative Concept  **Definition:** Ability to achieve reasonable accuracy and precision in determining a particular parameter when fitting a mathematical model to data.  **Synonyms:** - Identifiability
[C94928] Mathematical Integral -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A summation of many products, each being a successive value of a dependent variable y multiplied by a small increment delta x in the value of its independent variable.  **Synonyms:** - Integral
[C94929] Matrix Size -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** This is a quantifying property of a matrix that gives the total number of indexing values in each of its directions. For example matrix size would be specified as 2 x 3 if its 6 elements were distributed into 2 rows and 3 columns.
[C9493] Hypochondriasis -- **Semantic type:** Sign or Symptom  **Definition:** A somatoform disorder in which an individual is preoccupied with having a serious illness despite not having been given a corroborating diagnosis.  **Synonyms:** - Hypochondria
[C94930] Maximum Intensity Projection -- **Semantic type:** Manufactured Object  **Definition:** A 2D display that results when its pixels are given the activity concentration values that are solely from the largest voxel value encountered along each of the lines of sight perpendicular to this projection plane.  **Synonyms:** - MIP
[C94931] Maximum Pixel -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The largest activity concentration occurring for a pixel within a region of interest.
[C94932] Maximum Voxel -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The largest activity concentration occurring for a voxel within a volume of interest.
[C94933] Metabolic Rate Quantification -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A quantification method to determine metabolic rate.
[C94934] MicroPET -- **Semantic type:** Medical Device  **Definition:** A dedicated PET scanner with a small diameter ring of detectors that bring these close to small animal subjects.
[C94935] Molecular Imaging -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** The visualization, characterization and measurement of biological processes at the molecular and cellular levels in humans and other living systems. Molecular imaging typically includes two- or three-dimensional imaging as well as quantification over time. The techniques used include radiotracer imaging/nuclear medicine, magnetic resonance imaging, magnetic resonance spectroscopy, optical imaging, ultrasound and others.
[C94936] Monte Carlo Method -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** A computing method involving many similar individual, yet randomly differing, calculations. The individual calculations differ due to the introduction of random numbers for some input quantity or quantities.  **Synonyms:** - Monte Carlo
[C94937] Multi-planar Reformatting -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** Creating slice views from a 3D image with their planes having desired orientations.  **Synonyms:** - MPR
[C94938] Multiple Time Graphical Analysis -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** A method of obtaining descriptive parameters having physiological significance from plots whose points at various times are computed from dynamic scan data and possibly also tracer blood sampling. The most common methods here are Patlak plots and Logan plots.
[C94939] Myocardial Blood Flow -- **Semantic type:** Organ or Tissue Function  **Definition:** Perfusion of blood through the myocardium.  **Synonyms:** - MBF
[C9494] Latent Infection -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A period in which the infection is present in the host without producing overt symptoms.  (from Online Medical Dictionary)
[C94940] National Electrical Manufacturers Association -- **Semantic type:** Professional or Occupational Group  **Definition:** A leading trade association in the USA whose members are manufacturers of electro industry products. One of its useful functions is to provide agreed upon standards for equipment including medical devices.  **Synonyms:** - NEMA
[C94941] Noise Equivalent Count Rate -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** An adjusted PET scanner's count rate that results from a specified activity concentration within.  **Synonyms:** - NEC
[C94942] Non-linear Parameter Identification -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** A consequence of a mathematical model in which some parameters do not appear as separate data coefficients to the first power in the equations used to evaluate these from data fitting.
[C94943] Normalized Time -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** The integrated plasma concentration Cp of a tracer divided by the value of this concentration at the end of the integration time.
[C94944] Nyquist Frequency -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A frequency equal to half the sampling rate used in digitizing a continuously varying process.
[C94945] Parameter Identification -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** A process of finding model parameters that give a best fit of the model to the data points from a system.
[C94946] Parametric Image -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** An image where, instead of a customary parameter such as activity concentration, other computed results are displayed at each pixel (voxel) in their stead.
[C94947] Partial Volume Effect -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** The smearing out of a scanned object's true activity concentration that should appear at each pixel (voxel) into a wider displayed pattern because of the lack of perfect scanner resolution for small objects (i.e. having a dimension near or less than the scanner's full width at half maximum of its point spread function).
[C94948] Percent Injected Dose -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** Indicated as %ID, the amount of activity present in a region or organ as a % of total available injected activity or dose.
[C94949] PET Scanner 2D Mode -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** A measuring process with shielding septa restricting detectors' coincidences. It is generally referred to as simply 2D.  **Synonyms:** - 2D - 2D mode
[C9495] Radial Scar/Complex Sclerosing Lesion -- **Semantic type:** Disease or Syndrome  **Definition:** A benign breast lesion characterized by the presence of fibrosis with stellate appearance in the parenchyma.  The lesions are often multiple and bilateral.  Grossly, the lesions are irregular and firm and resemble carcinoma.  Microscopically, the stellate lesions are composed of dense, hyalinized collagen.  Entrapped in the collagen are tubules.  There is no evidence of cytologic atypia.  **Synonyms:** - Complex Sclerosing Lesion - Radial Scar - Radial Sclerosing Lesion
[C94950] PET Scanner 3D Mode -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** A measuring process without any shielding septa restricting detectors' coincidences. It is generally referred to as simply 3D.  **Synonyms:** - 3D - 3D mode
[C94951] Phase Analysis -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** Measuring successive phases of individual cycles in repetitive processes, such as the cardiac cycle, and then determining quantitative markers from this set.
[C94952] Pinhole Camera -- **Semantic type:** Medical Device  **Definition:** A special gamma camera having only one pinhole instead of multiple parallel holes as its collimator.
[C94953] Pixel Size -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The distance on the imaged subject that corresponds to the width (or the height typically the same as the width) of the smallest piece of the subject that is displayed as a pixel.
[C94954] Planar Imaging -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** Implementation of a gamma camera to obtain 2D images with no tomographic reconstruction process being involved.
[C94955] Point Spread Function -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** A spatial plot showing values in an image that result from an ideal point activity concentration source. The abscissa of this plot shows relative distance from the point's location.
[C94956] Polar Map -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** A 2-dimensional display in which the value plotted, such as by using a color scale, is located by its radial coordinate and an angular or azimuthal coordinate.
[C94957] Positron -- **Semantic type:** Element, Ion, or Isotope  **Definition:** The anti-particle of the electron, having the same mass but being positively rather than negatively charged.  **Synonyms:** - Positive beta particle
[C94958] Probability Density -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A quantifier used to characterize a statistical distribution. It describes the probability of a variable having a value within a small unit band of values around this value.
[C94959] Projection View -- **Semantic type:** Intellectual Product  **Definition:** Detector data acquired in a specified direction from the subject. It represents a view from a certain direction.
[C9496] T-Cell/Histiocyte-Rich Large B-Cell Lymphoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A large B-cell lymphoma characterized by the presence of a limited number of scattered neoplastic large B-lymphocytes which are admixed with numerous non-neoplastic T-lymphocytes and frequently histiocytes.  **Synonyms:** - T-Cell Rich/Histiocyte-Rich Large B-Cell Lymphoma - T-Cell/Histiocyte Rich Lymphoma - T-cell rich/histiocyte-rich large B-cell lymphoma - THRLBCL
[C94960] Quantitative Constraint -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** Mathematical conditions placed on some parameters in their determination from data.  **Synonyms:** - Constraint
[C94961] Radioactive Activity -- **Semantic type:** Conceptual Entity  **Definition:** Parameter used to quantify radioactivity and represents radioactive source transformations, i.e. disintegrations or emissions, per unit time.
[C94962] Radioactive Label -- **Semantic type:** Indicator, Reagent, or Diagnostic Aid  **Definition:** A substance containing a radioisotope that has an affinity for and binds to a chosen biologic substance of interest to enable quantification of biologic processes.  **Synonyms:** - Radioactive Tag - Radioactive Tracer
[C94963] Radioactive Source -- **Semantic type:** Qualitative Concept  **Definition:** The substance which is radioactive.
[C94964] Radioactive Tracer Kinetic Analysis -- **Semantic type:** Activity  **Definition:** An implementation of a compartmental model to quantify the time behavior of a tracer's uptake in a tissue.
[C94965] Radioactivity Cross Calibration -- **Semantic type:** Activity  **Definition:** The process of measuring a radioactivity sample in the scanner and a counter. The result is having a consistent measure of radioactivity from these 2 devices when both are used in a protocol.  **Synonyms:** - Cross Calibration
[C94966] Radionuclide Generator -- **Semantic type:** Manufactured Object  **Definition:** A device that produces a useful radionuclide from a parent substance contained within. As the parent decays a daughter, the useful radionuclide, is created and amounts may be obtained from the generator as needed.
[C94967] Rate Constant -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Parameter appearing in models to describe a unidirectional transfer rate out of a compartment. It is the (amount transferred per unit time)/(amount present in the compartment).  **Synonyms:** - Transfer Constant
[C94968] Rebinning -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** Sorting data into specially chosen classes or bins which differ from those pertaining to the original acquisition.
[C94969] Rectilinear Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** A very simplified scan, now of historical interest, in which a radiation detector is systematically positioned over the subject, viewing only a very small region at a time.  **Synonyms:** - Linear Scan
[C9497] Melanocytic Medulloblastoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A rare malignant embryonal neoplasm characterized by the presence of small cells which resemble the cells of classic medulloblastoma and a minor population of melanin-forming neuroepithelial cells.  It usually has an unfavorable clinical course.  **Synonyms:** - Medulloblastoma with Melanotic Differentiation - Medulloblastoma with Melanotic Differentiation - Melanotic Medulloblastoma - Melanotic medulloblastoma
[C94970] Reference Region -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** A region of interest in an image chosen for comparison purposes because its uptake properties have some desired feature.
[C94971] Relative Biological Effectiveness -- **Semantic type:** Conceptual Entity  **Definition:** A factor assigned to a type of ionizing radiation in converting absorbed dose measured in grays to a biologically equivalent dose measured in sieverts. In formula form, (equivalent dose) = (quality factor) x (absorbed dose).  **Synonyms:** - Quality Factor - RBE - Relative Biological Effectiveness (RBE)
[C94972] Robust -- **Semantic type:** Qualitative Concept  **Definition:** A desirable characteristic of a method in which its results would be rather unaffected by certain conditions under which these were obtained.
[C94973] Sagittal Plane -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** Pertaining to a plane containing a line parallel to the long axis of the subject's body, and additionally giving a left or right view. This plane is perpendicular to both the transaxial and coronal planes.  **Synonyms:** - Sagittal
[C94974] Scan Throughput -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The number of scans performed in some unit of time.
[C94975] Scan Time Frame -- **Semantic type:** Temporal Concept  **Definition:** A time interval within the total duration of a scan at one bed position during which detector data is acquired for an image. In the usual single scan there is only one frame at each bed position, with these occurring some chosen times after injection. In dynamic scans however there are a number of sequential frames at a fixed bed position.
[C94976] Scanner Camera Center of Rotation -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** An imaginary axis about which a scanner camera moves in its circular orbit.  **Synonyms:** - Center of Rotation
[C94977] Scanner Color Scale -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** The assignment of a collection of hues to quantitate numerical values being represented in a pixel (voxel) of an image.  **Synonyms:** - Color Scale
[C94978] Scanner Patient Bed Position -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** A designated location of the bed as it is moved through the scanner for purposes of positioning a desired part of the subject in the field of view.
[C94979] Scout Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** An initial scan with only the minimal detail needed to select the desired range of bed positions to be used in subsequent scans.
[C9498] Melanocytoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A usually benign neoplasm that arises from the sun-exposed skin, eye, and meninges. It is composed of spindle and/or epithelioid melanocytes and melanophages. It rarely progresses to melanoma.  **Synonyms:** - Magnocellular Nevus - Magnocellular nevus - Melanocytoma, NOS
[C94980] Semiquantitative Method -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** As colloquially used, a designation for a simpler quantitative method that contrasts with a more rigorous one used for a similar purpose. Often the terminology, semiquantitative analysis, is used in imaging as a synonym for usage of the standardized uptake value.
[C94981] Septa -- **Semantic type:** Medical Device  **Definition:** Parallel gamma absorbing plates close to detectors that restrict gamma reception from all directions from the scanned subject except in a somewhat narrow beam perpendicular to the subject's axis.
[C94982] Short Axis -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** Line through and perpendicular to an object's long axis. This line may intersect the long axis anywhere within the object.
[C94983] Signal to Noise Ratio -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The ratio of a measure of useful information from a system to that of a measure of interfering unwanted, often random, information that is also present.  **Synonyms:** - S/N - SNR
[C94984] Sinogram -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** A grayscale display of detector counts for various lines of response. These latter are located by the abscissa being their distance from the central axis of the scanner and their ordinate being their angle (0 to 180 degrees) relative to vertical.
[C94985] Sokoloff Model -- **Semantic type:** Intellectual Product  **Definition:** A widely used mathematical model for tracer uptake in PET. It describes inter-compartmental transports of glucose or its tracers.
[C94986] Specific Radioactive Activity -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A quantitative measure of the radioactive emissions per unit time per unit mass.
[C94987] Specific Radioactive Volume -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Volume contained in a unit mass. This is the reciprocal of the commonly encountered density, the mass within a unit volume.
[C94988] Statistical Parameter Mapping -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** Converting throughout a region the pixels' (voxels') activity concentrations to a difference from a reference and then normalized by a standard deviation.  **Synonyms:** - SPM
[C94989] Stroke Volume -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The difference in a cardiac ventricle's blood capacity taken at times of minimum and maximum blood content. This is end diastolic volume less end systolic volume.  **Synonyms:** - STROKVOL
[C9499] Melanomatosis -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process
[C94990] Talairach Space -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** A widely accepted standard coordinate system of brain physiologically-based regions, i.e. an atlas space, to which an individual brain geometry from a scan can be transformed and realigned for purposes of matching corresponding regions among brains.
[C94991] Topogram -- **Semantic type:** Laboratory or Test Result  **Definition:** A 2D projected image from a tomographic device that does not utilize its full capability of reconstructing into slices.
[C94992] Tracer Clearance -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** The process of a radioactive tracer leaving a tissue over time.  **Synonyms:** - Biological Clearance - Washout
[C94993] Tracer Extraction Fraction -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The probability of a tracer molecule being retained in a tissue once it has entered via the bloodstream. It can be calculated by: (tracer remaining in a tissue) / (total tracer that entered on a single passage of tracer-carrying blood through it).  **Synonyms:** - Extraction Fraction
[C94994] Transaxial Plane -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** Across (i.e. perpendicular to) the axial direction. When describing the plane of an image, it indicates the plane perpendicular to the long axis of the subject's body.  **Synonyms:** - Transaxial
[C94995] Transaxial Field of View -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** The diameter perpendicular to the body axis that can be imaged.
[C94996] Transient Ischemic Dilation -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The ratio of the left ventricular cavity volume during stress to the left ventricular cavity volume at rest.
[C94997] Transmission Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** A scan to obtain data for use in making attenuation corrections.
[C94998] Tumor to Normal -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** A common normalization of a tumor activity concentration, namely tumor activity concentration to normal tissue activity concentration.  **Synonyms:** - T/N
[C94999] Ventilation to Perfusion Ratio -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The ratio of activity concentration of a tracer measuring lung air flow per unit volume for a region to that of the activity concentration of a tracer measuring lung blood flow per unit volume for this region.  **Synonyms:** - V/Q
[C950] Anaxirone -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A synthetic triepoxide alkylating agent with potential antineoplastic activity. Anaxirone alkylates DNA via actual or derived epoxide groups, resulting in inhibition of DNA synthesis. This agent has been shown to exhibit a broad spectrum of antineoplastic activity against experimental tumors, including those resistant to other alkylating agents.  **Synonyms:** - 1,2,4-triglycidylurazol - ANAXIRONE - TGU - Triglycidylurazol
[C95000] Visual Interpretation -- **Semantic type:** Activity  **Definition:** Perusal of an image for information that can be gleaned from the relative intensities that display tracer uptake.
[C95001] Volume of Interest -- **Semantic type:** Conceptual Entity  **Definition:** A user-selected volume within a 3D image. The choice is guided by knowledge that activities concentrations for the voxels within the boundary contour of this region will be used in quantifying it.  **Synonyms:** - VOI
[C95002] Volume Rendering -- **Semantic type:** Occupational Activity  **Definition:** A 2D projection of a 3D image using one of a variety of algorithms that achieve a display of otherwise hidden voxel values.
[C95003] Voxel Size -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The three distances on the imaged subject that correspond to the width, height, and depth of the smallest piece of the subject that is displayed as a voxel.
[C95004] Whole Body Scan -- **Semantic type:** Diagnostic Procedure  **Definition:** A series of scans of a subject taken at sequential bed positions as the bed moves through the scanner. The images, each limited by an axial field of view, can then be pieced together to give an extended length image of the subject. A whole body scan contrasts with a single bed position scan in which only a rather limited region within the subject is to be studied.  **Synonyms:** - Whole-Body Imaging - Whole-Body Scan
[C95005] Zoom Factor -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The factor by which an image is given more detail in its display from a normal presentation in an original matrix.
[C95006] Binding Potential -- **Semantic type:** Molecular Function  **Definition:** A quantity used in tracer studies of receptor density in certain biological processes where this binding potential is proportional to the receptor density. Typically such processes involving transmitters and receptors would be modeled for interpreting dynamic scans.  **Synonyms:** - BP
[C95007] Effective Half-life -- **Semantic type:** Temporal Concept  **Definition:** Time for the amount of a radionuclide in a living organism to be reduced to half of its value at a given initial time. The amount diminishes due to 2 concurrent processes: radioactive decay, and clearance from the system.  **Synonyms:** - EFFHL - Effective Half-Life - Effective Half-Life
[C95008] Input Function -- **Semantic type:** Functional Concept  **Definition:** A time varying function providing causal changes to a system it acts upon.
[C95009] Ligand -- **Semantic type:** Biologically Active Substance  **Definition:** An ion, molecule or molecular group that binds to a substance to form a larger complex.
[C9501] Micrometastasis -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process
[C95010] Mean Life -- **Semantic type:** Phenomenon or Process  **Definition:** The time for a quantity to diminish by a factor of e = 2.718.
[C95011] Scanner Patient Bed -- **Semantic type:** Medical Device  **Definition:** A scanner's narrow horizontal platform on which the subject lies to be moved axially within the gantry in order to position appropriate subject sections within the scanner's field of view.
[C95012] Standardized Uptake Value Maximum -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** The maximum standardized uptake value among the pixels (voxels) within a region of interest.  **Synonyms:** - Maximum Standardized Uptake Value - SUVMAX - SUVmax - imaging suv max - imaging_suv_max
[C95013] Standardized Uptake Value Peak -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Standardized uptake value of the average for a limited number pixels (voxels) that includes the pixel (voxel) having SUVmax.  **Synonyms:** - Peak Standardized Uptake Value - SUVPEAK - SUVpeak
[C95014] Time Activity Curve -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Plot of activity concentration as the ordinate vs. time as the abscissa.  **Synonyms:** - TAC
[C95015] Vertical Long Axis of the Heart -- **Semantic type:** Spatial Concept  **Definition:** An oblique plane through the heart containing its long axis and showing 2 chambers. Also any plane parallel to this can be called a vertical long axis view.  **Synonyms:** - Vertical Long Axis
[C95016] Volume Curve -- **Semantic type:** Quantitative Concept  **Definition:** Plot of a ventricle volume as the ordinate vs. time in a cardiac cycle as the abscissa.
[C95017] Pregnancy and Lactation Status -- **Semantic type:** Finding  **Definition:** A finding regarding a female's pregnancy and lactation situation.  **Synonyms:** - FEMALE PHYSIOLOGICAL STATUS
[C95018] Use -- **Semantic type:** Activity  **Definition:** To put into action or service.  **Synonyms:** - Employ
[C95019] Disinfectant -- **Semantic type:** Hazardous or Poisonous Substance  **Synonyms:** - disinfectant
[C9502] Myolipoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A benign extrauterine tumor composed of mature adipocytes and smooth muscle cells.  **Synonyms:** - Extrauterine Lipoleiomyoma - Myolipoma of Soft Tissue
[C95020] Lutetium Lu 177 Dotatate -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A radioconjugate consisting of the tyrosine-containing somatostatin analog Tyr3-octreotate (TATE) conjugated with the bifunctional, macrocyclic chelating agent tetra-azacyclododecanetetra-acetic acid (DOTA) and radiolabeled with the beta-emitting radioisotope lutetium Lu 177, with potential imaging and antineoplastic activities. Lutetium Lu 177 dotatate binds to somatostatin receptors (SSTRs), with high affinity to type 2 SSTR, present on the cell membranes of many types of neuroendocrine tumor (NET) cells. Upon binding and internalization, this radioconjugate specifically delivers a cytotoxic dose of beta radiation to SSTR-positive cells. Tyr3-octreotate (TATE) is an octreotide derivative in which phenylalanine at position 3 is substituted by tyrosine and position 8 threoninol is replaced with threonine. SSTRs have been shown to be present in large numbers on NET and their metastases, while most other normal tissues express low levels of SSTRs.  **Synonyms:** - 177 Lu-DOTA-TATE - 177 Lu-DOTA-Tyr3-Octreotate - 177Lu-DOTA0-Tyr3-Octreotate - LUTETIUM OXODOTREOTIDE LU-177 - Lutathera - Lutetium (177Lu) Oxodotreotide - Lutetium Lu 177 DOTA(0)-Tyr(3)-Octreotate - Lutetium Lu 177 dotatate - Lutetium Lu 177-DOTA-Tyr3-Octreotate - Lutetium Oxodotreotide Lu-177 - lutetium Lu 177-DOTATATE
[C95021] Cinobufagin -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A bufadienolide compound extracted from the dried venom secreted by the parotid glands of toads and one of the glycosides in the traditional Chinese medicine ChanSu, with potential antineoplastic activity. Although the mechanism of action of cinobufagin is still under investigation, it has been found to suppress cancer cell proliferation and cause apoptosis in cancer cells via a sequence of apoptotic modulators that include mitochondrial Bax and cytosolic chromosome c, and caspases 3, 8, and 9. Possible upstream mediators of cinobufagin-induced apoptosis include Fas and p53.  **Synonyms:** - CINOBUFAGIN - Cinobufacini
[C95022] Cesium Cs-131 -- **Semantic type:** Element, Ion, or Isotope  **Definition:** An unstable radioisotope of cesium (Cs) with radiocytotoxic application. Cs-131 is a gamma photon-emitting radionuclide with high energy and a relatively short half-life of 9.7 days. When used in prostate brachytherapy, Cs-131 demonstrated advantages over other commonly used isotopes.  **Synonyms:** - 131-Cs - CESIUM CS-131
[C95023] Anti-human GITR Monoclonal Antibody TRX518 -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Immunologic Factor|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A humanized, Fc disabled anti-human glucocorticoid-induced tumor necrosis factor receptor (GITR) monoclonal antibody (MoAb) with immunomodulating activity. Anti-human GITR MoAb TRX518 blocks the interaction of GITR, found on multiple types of T cells, with its ligand, thereby inducing both the activation of tumor-antigen-specific T effector cells, as well as abrogating the suppression induced by inappropriately activated T regulatory cells. This agent is shown to act synergistically with chemotherapeutic drugs in multiple cancer models.  **Synonyms:** - Anti-huGITR MoAb TRX518 - TRX518
[C95024] Racotumomab -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Immunologic Factor|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An anti-idiotype murine monoclonal antibody (MoAb) specific to P3 MoAb with anti-metastatic effect. Racotumomab binds to the idiotype region of P3 MoAb and functionally mimics the three-dimensional structure of N-glycolyl ceramides of mono-sialyl lactose, the antigenic target of P3. As a result, this anti-idiotype antibody may stimulate the host immune system to elicit humoral and cellular immune responses against tumor cells expressing NeuGc-GM3 gangliosides, which are expressed in a wide variety of tumor cells.  **Synonyms:** - Anti-P3 Antibody Idiotype Monoclonal Antibody 1E10 - MoAb 1E10 - RACOTUMOMAB - Vaxira
[C95025] Iodine I 131 Monoclonal Antibody F16SIP -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A fully human monoclonal antibody (MoAb) against human A1 domain of tenascin-C, in small immunoprotein (SIP) format conjugated with iodine 131 with potential antineoplastic activity. Iodine I 131 MoAb F16SIP binds to tenascin-C on the vascular tissues and delivers cytotoxic radiation to the tumors, thereby minimizing systemic radiotoxicity. Tenascin-C is a glycoprotein of the extracellular matrix, and the large isoform of this matrix protein is expressed and restricted around vascular structures in the tumor stroma of a variety of different tumors.  **Synonyms:** - (131I) Monoclonal Antibody F16SIP - 131I-F16SIP - F16-131I - Tenarad
[C95026] Y-shape Branched Pegylated Filgrastim -- **Semantic type:** Immunologic Factor|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A long-acting, pegylated variant of filgrastim (recombinant human granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF)) with immunomodulating activity. Similar to endogenous G-CSF, this agent binds to and activates specific cell surface receptors, stimulating neutrophil progenitor proliferation and differentiation and selected neutrophil functions. The modification of this version of filgrastim with the addition of a Y-shaped branch of polyethylene glycol at a specific lysine residue (Lys 17) theoretically serves to extend the half-life of the agent.  **Synonyms:** - YPEG-filgrastim - YPEG-rhG-CSF
[C95027] Colorimetric Cell Viability Assay -- **Semantic type:** Research Activity  **Definition:** A colorimetric assay that can assess the viability of cells by quantitation of the reduction of a yellow tetrazolium salt substrate to a product that has a purple color. This assay can measure the cytotoxicity of a chemical or drug by determining the affect of treatment on cell viability.  **Synonyms:** - MTS Assay - MTT Assay
[C95028] PPP1R13L Gene -- **Semantic type:** Gene or Genome  **Definition:** This gene is involved in the regulation of both apoptosis and transcription.  **Synonyms:** - PPP1R13L - PPP1R13L - Protein Phosphatase 1, Regulatory (Inhibitor) Subunit 13 Like Gene

[C9506] Tegafur-Uracil -- **Semantic type:** Nucleic Acid, Nucleoside, or Nucleotide|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A formulated therapeutic oral agent consisting of a combination of the 5-fluorouracil (5-FU) congener prodrug tegafur (tetrahydrofuranyl-5-fluorouracil) and uracil (1:4). The high concentration of uracil reversibly inhibits the uracil-reducing enzyme dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD), thereby inhibiting first-pass DPD-mediated hepatic metabolism of the uracil analogue 5-FU and permitting administration of 5-FU as the orally bioavailable prodrug tegafur. Tegafur is bioactivated to 5-FU by liver microsomal cytochrome P450 enzymes. 5-FU is subsequently converted into its active metabolites 5-fluoro-deoxyuridine-monophosphate (FdUMP) and 5-fluorouridine-triphosphate (FUTP) intracellularly; these metabolites inhibit the enzyme thymidylate synthase and intercalate into RNA, resulting in decreased thymidine synthesis, reduced DNA synthesis, disrupted RNA function, and tumor cell cytotoxicity.  **Synonyms:** - Oral Fluorouracil-Uracil - TEGAFUR-URACIL - Tefudex - Tegafur and Uracil - Tegafur and uracil - UFT - UFT - Uftoral - Uftoral - Uftoral - Ufur - Uracil and Ftorafur - Uracil and Tegafur - Uracil and Tetrahydrofuranyl-5-Fluorouracil - Uracil/Tegafur (UFT) - tegafur-uracil

[C95076] Nightmare Disorder -- **Semantic type:** Mental or Behavioral Dysfunction  **Definition:** A sleep disorder characterized by the repeated occurrence of frightening dreams which precipitate awakenings from sleep; on awakening, the individual becomes fully alert and oriented and has detailed recall of the nightmare, which usually involves imminent danger or extreme embarrassment to the individual.

[C95080] Rapamycin-Polarized Th1/Tc1 Autologous T Lymphocytes -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Cell|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A population of T lymphocytes polarized by rapamycin with potential immunomodulating activity. The autologous T cells collected from the patient were co-stimulated with antibodies to the T-cell cell surface proteins CD3 and CD28 and expanded ex vivo in the presence of rapamycin, an immunosuppressive drug, and then infused back into the same patient. Both CD3 and CD28 are required for full T-cell activation. These lymphocytes expressed anti-apoptotic bcl-2 family member proteins (reduced Bax, Bak; increased phospho-Bad); maintained mitochondrial membrane potentials; and displayed reduced apoptosis. Adoptive transfer of this type of T cell potentially induces an anti-apoptotic Th1/Tc1 effector phenotype by promoting autophagy.  **Synonyms:** - ex vivo anti-CD3/CD28 Costimulated, Rapamycin-Polarized Th1/Tc1 Autologous T Lymphocytes

[C95123] Macimorelin -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An orally available synthetic mimetic of the growth hormone (GH) secretagogue ghrelin with potential anti-cachexia activity. Upon oral administration, macimorelin mimics endogenous ghrelin by stimulating appetite and binds to the growth hormone secretagogue receptor GHSR in the central nervous system, thereby mimicking the GH-releasing effects of ghrelin from the pituitary gland. Stimulation of GH secretion increases insulin-like growth factor-I (IGF-I) levels which may further stimulate protein synthesis. [C95148] Vosaroxin -- **Semantic type:** Antibiotic|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A small molecule and a naphthyridine analogue with antineoplastic activity. Vosaroxin intercalates into DNA in a site-specific manner and blocks the re-ligation process carried out by topoisomerase II during DNA replication. As a result, inhibition of DNA replication, RNA and protein synthesis occurs, followed by cell cycle arrest at G2 phase and induced p53-independent apoptosis. This agent shows a favorable toxicity profile in several aspects: it does not generate reactive oxygen species, as do anthracyclines, hence reducing the risk of cardiotoxicity; it is not a P-glycoprotein (P-gp) substrate, and thereby evades the common mechanism for multidrug resistance; and it has limited distribution to normal tissues and a more chemically stable molecular structure.  **Synonyms:** - 1,4-Dihydro-7-(3-methoxy-4-methylamino-1-pyrrolidinyl)-4-oxo-1-(2-thiazolyl)-1,8-naphthyridine-3-carboxylic acid - AG-7352 - SNS 595 - SNS-595 - SNS595 - SPC 595 - VOSAROXIN - Voreloxin

[C95169] Azidocillin Potassium -- **Semantic type:** Antibiotic|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** The potassium salt of a narrow-spectrum, semisynthetic penicillin derivative with antibacterial activity. Azidocillin binds to and inactivates penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located on the inner membrane of the bacterial cell wall. [C95198] Actinomycin C2 -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Antibiotic|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A natural analogue of actinomycin, a chromopeptide antineoplastic antibiotic isolated from the bacterial genus Streptomyces. Actinomycin C2 inhibits DNA replication as well as RNA and protein synthesis by various mechanisms including, intercalating into the minor groove of DNA and interfering with the function of topoisomerase II. In addition, actinomycin C2 appears to block the interaction between the SH2 domain of growth factor receptor-bound protein-2 (GRB2) and the Src homology 2 domain containing transforming protein 1 adaptor protein SHC, which plays a key role in the Ras signaling pathway thereby halting cellular differentiation and proliferation.  **Synonyms:** - ACTINOMYCIN C2 - Actinomycin D, 2a-D-Alloisoleucine-
[C95199] Carbon C-13 Dextromethorphan -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A radioconjugate consisting of dextromethorphan, a synthetic, methylated dextrorotatory analogue of levorphanol, conjugated with carbon-13 [(13)C] with radiotracer activity. (13)C-dextromethorphan can be used in a breath-test phenotype assay of CYP2D6 activity, based on the principle that CYP2D6-mediated O-demethylation cleaves a (13)CH3 that enters the body's carbon pool to be eliminated ultimately as (13)CO2 in expired air, which can be measured. The (13)C-dextromethorphan breath test may prove useful in identifying poor CYP2D6 metabolizers of such important clinical drugs as tamoxifen.  **Synonyms:** - 13C-dextromethorphan - [(13)C]-DM
[C952] Tesmilifene Hydrochloride -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Synonyms:** - BMS-217380-01 - BMY-33419 - Bis(diphenylphosphine)ethane - DPPE - DPPE - TESMILIFENE HYDROCHLORIDE

[C95200] Sodium Bicarbonate/Potassium Bicarbonate/Anhydrous Citric Acid -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A combination preparation containing sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, and anhydrous citric acid, with acid-neutralizing properties. This combination in water principally contains the antacids potassium citrate and sodium citrate, and is used for the relief of acid indigestion and heartburn. This combination does not contain aspirin, and therefore does not exert aspirin's analgesic or anti-inflammatory effects.  **Synonyms:** - Alka Seltzer Gold
[C95201] PARP Inhibitor AZD2461 -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An orally bioavailable inhibitor of the nuclear enzyme poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) with potential antineoplastic activity. PARP inhibitor AZD2461 selectively binds to PARP and prevents PARP-mediated DNA repair of single strand DNA breaks via the base-excision repair pathway. This enhances the accumulation of DNA strand breaks and promotes genomic instability and eventually leads to apoptosis. PARP catalyzes post-translational ADP-ribosylation of nuclear proteins that signal and recruit other proteins to repair damaged DNA and is activated by single-strand DNA breaks.  **Synonyms:** - AZD2461
[C95202] Pixatimod -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A synthetic sugar modified heparan sulfate mimetic and agonist of toll-like receptor 9 (TLR9), with potential immunostimulating, antineoplastic and anti-viral activities. Upon administration, pixatimod binds to and activates TLR9 expressed by dendritic cells (DCs) and B-cells. This initiates cytokine release from DCs and activates innate immune signaling pathways, and leads to the activation of natural killer (NK) cells to destroy tumor cells. The combination of pixatimod with certain checkpoint inhibitors may enhance the activation of cytotoxic T-lymphocytes to further kill tumor cells. In addition, pixatimod inhibits the cleavage of heparan sulfate from cell surface proteoglycan by heparanase (HPSE) and inhibits the infiltration of tumor-associated macrophages (TAMs). TLR9, a member of the TLR family, plays a fundamental role in pathogen recognition and activation of innate immunity.  **Synonyms:** - PG545 - PIXATIMOD
[C95203] c-Met Inhibitor AMG 337 -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An orally bioavailable inhibitor of the proto-oncogene c-Met with potential antineoplastic activity. c-Met inhibitor AMG 337 selectively binds to c-Met, thereby disrupting c-Met signal transduction pathways. This may induce cell death in tumor cells overexpressing c-Met protein or expressing constitutively activated c-Met protein. c-Met protein, the product of the proto-oncogene c-Met, is a receptor tyrosine kinase also known as hepatocyte growth factor receptor (HGFR); this protein is overexpressed or mutated in many tumor cell types and plays key roles in tumor cell proliferation, survival, invasion, and metastasis, and tumor angiogenesis.  **Synonyms:** - 1,6-Naphthyridin-5(6H)-one, 6-((1R)-1-(8-fluoro-6-(1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-1,2,4-triazolo(4,3-a)pyridin-3-yl)ethyl)-3-(2-methoxyethoxy)- - AMG 337 - AMG-337 - AMG337

BR55 is prepared by incorporation of a biospecific heterodimer peptide into the perfluorobutane-containing lipid-shelled microbubble membrane; the peptide specifically binds to VEGFR2 expressed on tumoral vascular endothelium allowing ultrasound imaging of the sites of active angiogenesis.  **Synonyms:** - BR55

[C95206] Depatuxizumab -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Immunologic Factor|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A humanized monoclonal antibody (MoAb) against human epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) with antineoplastic activity. Depatuxizumab targets the EGFR deletion variant, de2-7 EGFR as well as wild-type EGFR expressed in cells overexpressing the receptor, thereby preventing the activation and subsequent dimerization of the receptor; the decrease in receptor activation and dimerization result in an inhibition in signal transduction and anti-proliferative effects. This MoAb targets cells expressing aberrant EGFR, hence making it an ideal candidate for generation of radioisotope or toxin conjugates.  **Synonyms:** - ABT-806 - DEPATUXIZUMAB - Immunoglobulin G1, Anti-(Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor) (Human-mus musculus Monoclonal ABT-806 Heavy Chain), Disulfide with Human-mus musculus Monoclonal ABT-806 Light Chain, Dimer - anti-EGFR MoAb ABT-806 - anti-EGFR mAb ABT-806 - mAb-806
[C95207] HLA-A*2402-Restricted CDCA1-URLC10-KIF20A-DEPDC1-MPHOSPH1 Multipeptide Vaccine -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Immunologic Factor|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A cancer vaccine containing five HLA-A*2402-restricted peptide epitopes with potential immunostimulatory and antitumor activities. Peptide epitopes in this vaccine are derived from CDCA1 (cell division cycle-associated protein 1), URLC10 (up-regulated lung cancer 10), KIF20A (kinesin-like family member 20A), DEPDC1 (DEP domain containing 1), and MPHOSPH1 (M phase phosphoprotein 1). Upon administration, this peptide vaccine may stimulate a cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL) response against CDCA1-,URLC10-,KIF20A-,DEPDC1-, or MPHOSPH1-expressing tumor cells, resulting in tumor cell lysis and decreased tumor growth. HLA-A*2402 is an MHC class I molecule that presents antigenic peptides to CD8+ T cells; epitope design restricted to epitopes that bind most efficiently to HLA-A*2402 may improve antigenic peptide immunogenicity.
[C95208] Triazene Derivative TriN2755 -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A synthetic triazene derivative with antineoplastic activity. Upon metabolic activation via N-demethylation, TriN2755 is converted into highly reactive carbocations that can alkylate DNA and other macromolecules, thereby resulting in DNA cross links, inhibiting DNA replication and repair, and subsequently inducing apoptosis. This agent has high hydrophilicity and photostability and shows a favorable toxicity profile over the other triazenes.  **Synonyms:** - TRIN-2755 - TriN2755
[C95209] Guadecitabine -- **Semantic type:** Nucleic Acid, Nucleoside, or Nucleotide|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A dinucleotide antimetabolite composed of a decitabine linked via phosphodiester bond to a deoxyguanosine, with potential antineoplastic activity. Following metabolic activation via cleavage of the phosphodiester bond and incorporation of the decitabine moiety into DNA, guadecitabine inhibits DNA methyltransferase, thereby causing non-specific, genome-wide hypomethylation, and induction of cell cycle arrest at S-phase. This agent is resistant to cytidine deaminase, which may result in gradual release of decitabine both extra- and intra-cellularly, leading to prolonged exposure to decitabine.  **Synonyms:** - DNMT inhibitor SGI-110 - GUADECITABINE - S110 - SGI 110 - SGI-110 - SGI110

[C95210] Clevudine -- **Semantic type:** Nucleic Acid, Nucleoside, or Nucleotide|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A synthetic pyrimidine analogue with activity against hepatitis B virus (HBV). Intracellularly, clevudine is phosphorylated to its active metabolites, clevudine monophosphate and triphosphate. The triphosphate metabolite competes with thymidine for incorporation into viral DNA, thereby causing DNA chain termination and inhibiting the function of HBV DNA polymerase (reverse transcriptase). Clevudine has a long half-life and shows significant reduction of covalently closed circular DNA (cccDNA), therefore the patient is less likely to have a relapse after treatment is discontinued.  **Synonyms:** - 1-(2-Deoxy-2-fluoro-beta-L-arabinofuranosyl)-5-methyl-2,4(1H,3H)-pyrimidinedione - 1-(2-Deoxy-2-fluoro-beta-L-arabinofuranosyl)thymine - 2'-Fluoro-5-methyl-beta-L-arabinofuranosyluracil - CLEVUDINE - L-FMAU - Levovir - Revovir

[C95214] Ozarelix -- **Semantic type:** Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A highly modified, fourth generation linear decapeptide with gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH or LHRH) antagonizing properties. Ozarelix competitively binds to and blocks the gonadotropin releasing hormone receptor in the anterior pituitary gland, thereby inhibiting the secretion and release of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle stimulating hormone (FSH). In males, the inhibition of LH secretion prevents the release of testosterone. [C95223] Nilotinib Hydrochloride Anhydrous -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** The hydrochloride salt of nilotinib, an orally bioavailable aminopyrimidine-derivative Bcr-Abl tyrosine kinase inhibitor with antineoplastic activity. [C95349] Study Day Range -- **Semantic type:** Temporal Concept  **Definition:** The relative timing for a planned or performed activity expressed as the number of days offset from the study-defined reference activity (e.g., date of registration, start of treatment) for this particular experimental unit. [C95397] Intramucosal Adenocarcinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neoplastic lesion that shows morphologic evidence of invasion into the lamina propria or muscularis mucosa.  There is no evidence of invasion into the submucosa. [C95595] Pancreatic Somatostatinoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A neuroendocrine tumor arising from the delta cells of the pancreas. [C95597] Pancreatic Glucagonoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A usually malignant, glucagon-producing neuroendocrine tumor arising from the pancreatic alpha cells. It may be associated with necrolytic erythema migrans, diarrhea, diabetes, glossitis, weight loss, malabsorption, and anemia. Almost 25% of patients experience thromboembolic complications, and 50% have liver metastases at the time of diagnosis.  **Synonyms:** - Glucagonoma - glucagonoma

[C95599] Pancreatic Vipoma -- **Semantic type:** Neoplastic Process  **Definition:** A usually malignant pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor producing vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP).  It is associated with watery diarrhea, hypokalemia, and hypochlorhydria or achlorhydria.  One third of cases are metastatic at the time of diagnosis.
[C956] Edelfosine -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A synthetic analog of lysophosphatidylcholine, an ether lipid, possessing anti-leishmanial and antineoplastic activity.  The mechanism of action for edelfosine has not been fully elucidated.  Targeting cellular membranes, edelfosine modulates membrane permeability, membrane lipid composition, and phospholipid metabolism.  Edelfosine also inhibits the phosphatidylinositol 3 kinase (PI3K)-AKT/PKB survival pathway, possibly activating the pro-apoptotic stress-activated protein kinase (SAPK/JNK) pathways, thereby inducing apoptosis.  In addition, this agent inhibits protein kinase C, intracellular activation of the Fas/CD95 receptor, and intracellular acidification.  Anti-leishmanial activity may be due to inhibition of a glycosomal alkyl-specific-acylCoA acyltransferase. (NCI)  **Synonyms:** - 1-O-Octadecyl-2-O-methyl-rac-glycero-3-phosphocholine - 1-Octadecyl-2-methoxy-rac-glycero-3-phosphocholine - EDELFOSINE - ET-18-OCH3 - Edelfosinum - Et-18-OCH3

[C95699] TLR7 Agonist ANA773 Tosylate -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** The tosylate salt form of ANA773, a Toll-Like Receptor 7 (TLR7) agonist prodrug with potential immunostimulating activity. Upon oral administration, ANA773 is metabolized into its active form that binds to and activates TLR7, thereby stimulating dendritic cells (DCs) and enhancing natural killer cell (NK) cytotoxicity. This activation results in the production of proinflammatory cytokines, including interferon alpha, and enhanced antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC). TLR7 is a member of the TLR family, which plays a fundamental role in pathogen recognition and activation of innate immunity.  **Synonyms:** - ANA773 Tosylate - Toll-Like Receptor 7 Agonist ANA773 Tosylate
[C957] 10-Deacetyltaxol -- **Semantic type:** Organic Chemical|Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** An analog of paclitaxel with antineoplastic activity.  10-Deacetyltaxol binds to and stabilizes the resulting microtubules, thereby inhibiting microtubule disassembly which results in cell- cycle arrest at the G2/M phase and apoptosis.  **Synonyms:** - 10-DEACETYLPACLITAXEL - 10-Deacetylpaclitaxel - benzenepropanoic acid, beta-(benzoylamino)-alpha-hydroxy-, 12b-(acetyloxy)-12-(benzoyloxy)-2a,3,4,4a,5,6,9,10,11,12,12a,12b-dodecahydro-4,6,11-trihydroxy-4a,8,13,13-tetramethyl-5-oxo-7,11-methano-1H-cyclodeca(3,4)benz(1,2-b)oxet-9-yl ester, (2aR-(2aalpha,4beta,4abeta,6beta,9alpha(alphaR*,betaS*),11alpha,12alpha,12aalpha,12balpha))-

[C95700] Anticachexia Agent MT-102 -- **Semantic type:** Pharmacologic Substance  **Definition:** A small molecule with potential anticachexia activity.
